items.title: Tree protester warned not to reoffend says she would 'absolutely' do it again

children.content: Activist and author Mij Tanith refuses to rule out being arrested again for protesting against the felling of trees in the Adelaide Park Lands, despite a warning from a magistrate she could face a conviction next time.

items.title: Governments warned about $240m Nyrstar subsidy payouts

children.content: A group of international shareholders are warning state and federal governments against giving global commodities giant Trafigura hundreds of millions of dollars to keep smelting operations going in Australia.

image.altText: Port Pirie lead smelter

items.title: Explosion in feral pig numbers attributed to major flooding in far north SA

children.content: Feral pig numbers are exploding in outback South Australia after record-breaking floods, prompting a call from station owners and the Invasive Species Council for governments to take action.

image.altText: A mob of feral pigs wading across and out of flood waters

items.title: Man rescued from flood waters as SA wild weather leaves trail of damage

children.content: Storm damage have prompted extensive clean-ups across South Australia while a man had to be rescued from his vehicle's rooftop after he tried to drive through flood waters.

gallery.altText: SES volunteers in orange suits gather around white emergency ahead of the rescue of a man stranded in floodwater.

thumbnail.altText: SES volunteers in orange suits gather around white emergency ahead of the rescue of a man stranded in floodwater.

items.title: Remembering 'Miss Stayput's' legendary stand against SA's largest shopping mall

children.content: Eva Phelps was 82 when she refused to sell her land for the development of Marion Shopping Centre, so it was built around her, making her small house a veritable castle.

image.altText: An office building with Westfield written at the top

items.title: MP calls for investigation into anti-abortion campaigner

children.content: Anti-abortion campaigner Joanna Howe has been called out for potentially breaching campaign financing laws, with one MP labelling her "attack" style as harmful and dangerous.

image.altText: Joanna Howe stands next to women holding up ban feticide sign.

items.title: School worker sexually assaulted girls in Adelaide Hills, court told

children.content: A contract worker accused  of indecently assaulted two students at an Adelaide Hills school he worked at has appeared in court for the first time.

image.altText: A sign that says "Mount Barker Courthouse" stands outside a brick building.

items.title: Major milk company fined for misleading consumers about fresh milk

children.content: One of Australia's largest milk providers has been fined by the consumer watchdog for misleading consumers with "fresh milk" labels on products that contained substantial amounts of powdered milk.

image.altText: Milk pouring into a cup with soft green background

items.title: Arsonist with 'fixated discontent' jailed over $1.5m hotel fire

children.content: A judge has jailed a man who "embarked on a consistent campaign of terror" against his former business partner, including torching the complex containing the Oxford Hotel in North Adelaide.

image.altText: old hotel on fire

items.title: Fans hope to see Thunderbirds win netball grand final for third time

children.content: Among the devoted Thunderbirds fans making the journey to Melbourne for the grand final against the Vixens are the Rawlins family, including sisters Tess and Eve.

image.altText: Supporters of the Adelaide Thunderbirds.

items.title: Wettest year on record sees controversial and highly flammable weed 'explode'

children.content: As buffel grass explodes across Central Australia due to high rainfall, there are fears it will spread further east and threaten landmarks in South Australia and other states.

image.altText: A woman holding a large bunch of grass.

items.title: Adelaide gets 70pc of July rain in one day with windy weather to continue

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology says metropolitan Adelaide has received 70 per cent of its median rainfall for July in a single day, as South Australian farmers welcome the conditions.

image.altText: water overflow from a grass patch over a short wall to a busy road

items.title: Why Alice Springs has been dubbed the lesbian capital of Australia

children.content: New research places Alice Springs in the top 10 most inclusive Australian cities for LGBTQIA+ people, cementing the outback town's long-held reputation as the lesbian capital of the country.

children.content: A 12-year-old boy who allegedly had a machete concealed in his pants was among four boys arrested in Rundle Mall over two days.

items.title: As main street shops vanish, could they help solve housing shortages?

children.content: As businesses shut up shop in regional main streets, the government is weighing up whether they could help solve the housing crisis.

items.title: Man who spent eight days stuck under collapsed shopping centre in Venezuela rescued alive

children.content: Hernan Alberto Gil Flores, a 43-year-old Venezuelan man was dug out alive from under the ruins of a collapsed shopping centre, eight days after devastating earthquakes shook the nation.

articles.title: Micro 'cockroach' drones help find earthquake survivors in Venezuela

items.title: 'Advocate for yourself': Woman's warning after partner's cancer death

items.title: Ohio town in shock after 16 kids found in squalor 'right under our noses'

items.title: If the odds are anything to go by, this 104yo WWII veteran should not be alive

children.content: Eighty five years ago, Brian Walley, a prisoner of war in a German camp after crashing his bomber into the North Sea, doubted he would live for long. Yesterday, he celebrated his 104th birthday.

items.title: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt?

children.content: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt and win their first ever knockout final at a World Cup?
The Pharaohs have Salah, Marmoush and a dangerous counterattack, but the Socceroos have the physical edge, aerial threats and set-piece power to cause serious problems.
We take a closer look Egypt's strengths, and why Australia are a dangerous underdog that shouldn't be written off.

items.title: Concussion awareness leaving parents 'terrified', experts say
